Happy.text: So remember Me; I will remember you. And be grateful to Me and do not deny Me.
Happy.author: Quran

Happy.text: If you are grateful, I will surely increase you.
Happy.author: Quran

Happy.text: Indeed, with hardship comes ease.
Happy.author: Quran

Happy.text: Indeed, with hardship comes ease.
Happy.author: Quran

Happy.text: Say, 'In the bounty of Allah and in His mercy—in that let them rejoice.'
Happy.author: Quran

Happy.text: Whoever relies upon Allah, then He is sufficient for him.
Happy.author: Quran

Happy.text: Indeed, Allah is with the patient.
Happy.author: Quran

Happy.text: Indeed, the allies of Allah—there will be no fear concerning them, nor will they grieve.
Happy.author: Quran

Happy.text: Do not despair of the mercy of Allah.
Happy.author: Quran

Happy.text: Allah does not burden a soul beyond what it can bear.
Happy.author: Quran

Happy.text: Whoever does righteousness, whether male or female, while being a believer—We will surely grant them a good life.
Happy.author: Quran

Happy.text: Indeed, the mercy of Allah is near to the doers of good.
Happy.author: Quran

Happy.text: And your Lord is going to give you, and you will be satisfied.
Happy.author: Quran

Happy.text: He found you lost and guided you.
Happy.author: Quran

Happy.text: So as for the favor of your Lord, proclaim it.
Happy.author: Quran

Happy.text: The believers are but brothers.
Happy.author: Quran

Happy.text: Whoever fears Allah—He will make for him a way out and provide for him from where he does not expect.
Happy.author: Quran

Happy.text: Indeed, my Lord is near and responsive.
Happy.author: Quran

Happy.text: Call upon Me; I will respond to you.
Happy.author: Quran

Happy.text: Indeed, Allah loves those who rely upon Him.
Happy.author: Quran

Happy.text: The strong believer is better and more beloved to Allah than the weak believer, though there is good in both.
Happy.author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

Happy.text: None of you truly believes until he loves for his brother what he loves for himself.
Happy.author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

Happy.text: The best of people are those who are most beneficial to people.
Happy.author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

Happy.text: Allah is gentle and loves gentleness in all matters.
Happy.author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

Happy.text: Smile at your brother, for it is charity.
Happy.author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

Happy.text: The most beloved deeds to Allah are those done regularly, even if they are few.
Happy.author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

Happy.text: The merciful are shown mercy by the Most Merciful. Be merciful to those on earth, and the One above the heavens will have mercy upon you.
Happy.author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

Happy.text: Purity is half of faith.
Happy.author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

Happy.text: Whoever believes in Allah and the Last Day should speak good or remain silent.
Happy.author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

Happy.text: The nearest of people to me on the Day of Resurrection will be those with the best character.
Happy.author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

Sad.text: And We will surely test you with something of fear and hunger and a loss of wealth and lives and fruits, but give good tidings to the patient.
Sad.author: Quran

Sad.text: Those who, when disaster strikes them, say, 'Indeed we belong to Allah, and indeed to Him we will return.'
Sad.author: Quran

Sad.text: Those are the ones upon whom are blessings from their Lord and mercy. And it is those who are rightly guided.
Sad.author: Quran

Sad.text: Indeed, with hardship comes ease.
Sad.author: Quran

Sad.text: Indeed, with hardship comes ease.
Sad.author: Quran

Sad.text: Allah does not burden a soul beyond what it can bear.
Sad.author: Quran

Sad.text: Do not despair of the mercy of Allah. Indeed, Allah forgives all sins.
Sad.author: Quran

Sad.text: Indeed, Allah is with the patient.
Sad.author: Quran

Sad.text: And seek help through patience and prayer.
Sad.author: Quran

Sad.text: And seek help through patience and prayer. Indeed, Allah is with the patient.
Sad.author: Quran

Sad.text: My mercy encompasses all things.
Sad.author: Quran

Sad.text: Indeed, my Lord is near and responsive.
Sad.author: Quran

Sad.text: Whoever puts his trust in Allah, then He is sufficient for him.
Sad.author: Quran

Sad.text: And He found you lost and guided you.
Sad.author: Quran

Sad.text: And your Lord is going to give you, and you will be satisfied.
Sad.author: Quran

Sad.text: Your Lord has not forsaken you, nor has He become displeased.
Sad.author: Quran

Sad.text: Call upon Me; I will respond to you.
Sad.author: Quran

Sad.text: Indeed, Allah loves those who are constantly repentant.
Sad.author: Quran

Sad.text: Indeed, the friends of Allah—there will be no fear concerning them, nor will they grieve.
Sad.author: Quran

Sad.text: So be patient. Indeed, the promise of Allah is truth.
Sad.author: Quran

Sad.text: No fatigue, illness, anxiety, sorrow, harm, or distress befalls a Muslim—even the prick of a thorn—except that Allah expiates some of his sins because of it.
Sad.author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

Sad.text: How wonderful is the affair of the believer. If something good happens to him, he is grateful; if hardship befalls him, he is patient, and that is good for him.
Sad.author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

Sad.text: Whoever remains patient, Allah will grant him patience. No one is given a gift better and more comprehensive than patience.
Sad.author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

Sad.text: Allah does not look at your appearance or wealth, but He looks at your hearts and your deeds.
Sad.author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

Sad.text: The people who are tested most severely are the Prophets, then those nearest to them in righteousness.
Sad.author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

Sad.text: When Allah intends good for someone, He afflicts him with trials.
Sad.author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

Sad.text: The supplication of the distressed is answered when he calls upon Allah.
Sad.author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ
Sad.source: Meaning supported by authentic narrations on accepted supplication

Sad.text: Be mindful of Allah, and you will find Him before you. Know that victory comes with patience, relief with affliction, and ease with hardship.
Sad.author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

Sad.text: The most beloved deeds to Allah are those done consistently, even if they are few.
Sad.author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

Sad.text: Whoever relieves a believer of a hardship in this world, Allah will relieve him of a hardship on the Day of Resurrection.
Sad.author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

Lost.text: Guide us to the straight path.
Lost.author: Quran

Lost.text: This is the Book about which there is no doubt, a guidance for those conscious of Allah.
Lost.author: Quran

Lost.text: Allah is the ally of those who believe. He brings them out from darkness into the light.
Lost.author: Quran

Lost.text: Indeed, this Qur'an guides to that which is most upright.
Lost.author: Quran

Lost.text: Whoever relies upon Allah, then He is sufficient for him.
Lost.author: Quran

Lost.text: And He found you lost and guided you.
Lost.author: Quran

Lost.text: Indeed, Allah is with those who fear Him and those who are doers of good.
Lost.author: Quran

Lost.text: Indeed, my Lord is near and responsive.
Lost.author: Quran

Lost.text: Call upon Me; I will respond to you.
Lost.author: Quran

Lost.text: And whoever fears Allah—He will make for him a way out.
Lost.author: Quran

Lost.text: Indeed, Allah commands justice, excellence, and giving to relatives.
Lost.author: Quran

Lost.author: Quran

Lost.author: Quran

Lost.author: Quran

Lost.text: Say, 'My Lord, increase me in knowledge.'
Lost.author: Quran

Lost.text: The best among you are those who learn the Qur'an and teach it.
Lost.author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

Lost.text: Whoever travels a path seeking knowledge, Allah will make easy for him a path to Paradise.
Lost.author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

Lost.text: Be mindful of Allah, and He will protect you. Be mindful of Allah, and you will find Him before you.
Lost.author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

Lost.text: Tie your camel and then put your trust in Allah.
Lost.author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

Lost.text: The most beloved places to Allah are the mosques.
Lost.author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

Lost.text: Religion is sincere advice.
Lost.author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

Lost.text: Whoever follows a path in search of knowledge, Allah will make his path to Paradise easy.
Lost.author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

Lost.text: The believer who mixes with people and is patient with their harm is better than the one who does not mix with them.
Lost.author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

Lost.text: Ask Allah for guidance and soundness.
Lost.author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

Lost.text: The most beloved speech to Allah is: 'SubhanAllahi wa bihamdihi.'
Lost.author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

Lost.text: Actions are judged by intentions.
Lost.author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

Lost.text: Whoever seeks the pleasure of Allah, Allah will suffice him against the people.
Lost.author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

Lost.text: The wise person is the one who holds himself accountable and works for what comes after death.
Lost.author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

Lost.text: Consult your heart, even if people continue giving you their opinions.
Lost.author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

Lost.text: O Allah, I ask You for guidance, piety, chastity, and self-sufficiency.
Lost.author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

Lonely.text: And when My servants ask you concerning Me—indeed I am near. I respond to the invocation of the supplicant when he calls upon Me.
Lonely.author: Quran

Lonely.text: Indeed, Allah is with the patient.
Lonely.author: Quran

Lonely.text: Indeed, I am with you both; I hear and I see.
Lonely.author: Quran

Lonely.text: Do not grieve; indeed Allah is with us.
Lonely.author: Quran

Lonely.text: We are closer to him than his jugular vein.
Lonely.author: Quran

Lonely.text: And He is with you wherever you are.
Lonely.author: Quran

Lonely.text: Indeed, in the remembrance of Allah do hearts find rest.
Lonely.author: Quran

Lonely.text: Indeed, Allah is the ally of those who believe.
Lonely.author: Quran

Lonely.text: The believers are but brothers.
Lonely.author: Quran

Lonely.text: My mercy encompasses all things.
Lonely.author: Quran

Lonely.text: Your Lord has not forsaken you, nor has He become displeased.
Lonely.author: Quran

Lonely.text: And your Lord is going to give you, and you will be satisfied.
Lonely.author: Quran

Lonely.text: Call upon Me; I will respond to you.
Lonely.author: Quran

Lonely.text: Indeed, the mercy of Allah is near to the doers of good.
Lonely.author: Quran

Lonely.text: Whoever relies upon Allah, then He is sufficient for him.
Lonely.author: Quran

Lonely.text: The example of the believers in their mutual love, mercy and compassion is like one body; if one part suffers, the whole body responds with wakefulness and fever.
Lonely.author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

Lonely.text: A believer to another believer is like a building whose different parts support each other.
Lonely.author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

Lonely.text: Allah continues to help the servant as long as the servant helps his brother.
Lonely.author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

Lonely.text: Whoever relieves a believer of a hardship in this world, Allah will relieve him of a hardship on the Day of Resurrection.
Lonely.author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

Lonely.text: None of you truly believes until he loves for his brother what he loves for himself.
Lonely.author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

Lonely.text: The merciful are shown mercy by the Most Merciful. Be merciful to those on earth, and the One above the heavens will have mercy upon you.
Lonely.author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

Lonely.text: The Muslim is the brother of another Muslim. He neither oppresses him nor abandons him.
Lonely.author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

Lonely.text: The most beloved places to Allah are the mosques.
Lonely.author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

Lonely.text: The closest people to Allah on the Day of Resurrection are those with the best character.
Lonely.author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

Lonely.text: Exchange gifts, for they increase love between you.
Lonely.author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

Lonely.text: Whoever visits a sick person or visits his brother for the sake of Allah, a caller announces: 'May you be blessed, may your walking be blessed, and may you occupy a lofty place in Paradise.'
Lonely.author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

Lonely.text: The believer who mixes with people and patiently bears their harm is better than the believer who does not mix with people.
Lonely.author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

Lonely.text: The best of companions in the sight of Allah is the one who is best to his companion.
Lonely.author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

Lonely.text: Spread peace among yourselves.
Lonely.author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

Lonely.text: Whoever believes in Allah and the Last Day should maintain the ties of kinship.
Lonely.author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

Angry.text: Those who spend in prosperity and adversity, who restrain anger and pardon people—and Allah loves the doers of good.
Angry.author: Quran

Angry.text: Repel evil with that which is better.
Angry.author: Quran

Angry.text: The good deed and the evil deed are not equal. Repel evil with that which is better, and the one between whom and you was enmity will become as though he were a devoted friend.
Angry.author: Quran

Angry.text: Indeed, Allah commands justice, excellence, and giving to relatives.
Angry.author: Quran

Angry.text: Indeed, Allah is with the patient.
Angry.author: Quran

Angry.text: And be patient. Indeed, Allah does not allow the reward of the doers of good to be lost.
Angry.author: Quran

Angry.text: Indeed, Allah loves those who act justly.
Angry.author: Quran

Angry.text: The servants of the Most Merciful are those who walk upon the earth humbly, and when the ignorant address them harshly, they say words of peace.
Angry.author: Quran

Angry.text: Speak kindly to people.
Angry.author: Quran

Angry.text: Indeed, Allah loves those who rely upon Him.
Angry.author: Quran

Angry.text: Whoever is patient and forgives—indeed, that is among the matters requiring determination.
Angry.author: Quran

Angry.text: The recompense of an evil is an evil like it, but whoever pardons and makes reconciliation, his reward is with Allah.
Angry.author: Quran

Angry.text: Indeed, Allah does not like the wrongdoing people.
Angry.author: Quran

Angry.text: And lower your wing to the believers.
Angry.author: Quran

Angry.text: The believers are but brothers, so make peace between your brothers.
Angry.author: Quran

Angry.text: The strong man is not the one who can overpower others. Rather, the strong man is the one who controls himself when angry.
Angry.author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

Angry.text: Do not become angry.
Angry.author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

Angry.text: Whoever restrains his anger while being able to act upon it, Allah will call him before all creation on the Day of Resurrection.
Angry.author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

Angry.text: The merciful are shown mercy by the Most Merciful. Show mercy to those on the earth, and the One above the heavens will show mercy to you.
Angry.author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

Angry.text: The believer is not one who insults others, curses others, speaks indecently, or is foul-mouthed.
Angry.author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

Angry.text: Whoever believes in Allah and the Last Day should speak good or remain silent.
Angry.author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

Angry.text: The best of you are those with the best character.
Angry.author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

Angry.text: Allah is gentle and loves gentleness in all matters.
Angry.author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

Angry.text: Gentleness is not found in anything except that it beautifies it, and it is not removed from anything except that it makes it defective.
Angry.author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

Angry.text: The most beloved of you to me and the nearest to me on the Day of Resurrection are those with the best manners.
Angry.author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

Angry.text: Do not hate one another, do not envy one another, do not turn away from one another. Be servants of Allah as brothers.
Angry.author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

Angry.text: A Muslim is the brother of another Muslim. He neither oppresses him nor abandons him.
Angry.author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

Angry.text: When one of you becomes angry while standing, let him sit down. If the anger leaves him, well and good; otherwise, let him lie down.
Angry.author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

Angry.text: Anger is from Satan. If one of you becomes angry, let him perform ablution (wudu).
Angry.author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

Angry.text: The most perfect of the believers in faith are those with the best character.
Angry.author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

Afraid.text: Do not fear; indeed, I am with you both. I hear and I see.
Afraid.author: Quran

Afraid.text: Do not grieve; indeed Allah is with us.
Afraid.author: Quran

Afraid.text: Indeed, the allies of Allah—there will be no fear concerning them, nor will they grieve.
Afraid.author: Quran

Afraid.text: Whoever relies upon Allah—then He is sufficient for him.
Afraid.author: Quran

Afraid.text: Allah is sufficient for us, and He is the best Disposer of affairs.
Afraid.author: Quran

Afraid.text: It is only Satan who frightens his supporters. So do not fear them, but fear Me, if you are believers.
Afraid.author: Quran

Afraid.text: And whoever fears Allah—He will make for him a way out.
Afraid.author: Quran

Afraid.text: And He will provide for him from where he does not expect.
Afraid.author: Quran

Afraid.text: Indeed, Allah defends those who believe.
Afraid.author: Quran

Afraid.text: Call upon Me; I will respond to you.
Afraid.author: Quran

Afraid.text: And when My servants ask you concerning Me—indeed I am near.
Afraid.author: Quran

Afraid.text: Allah does not burden a soul beyond what it can bear.
Afraid.author: Quran

Afraid.text: Indeed, with hardship comes ease.
Afraid.author: Quran

Afraid.text: Indeed, with hardship comes ease.
Afraid.author: Quran

Afraid.text: Indeed, my Lord is near and responsive.
Afraid.author: Quran

Afraid.text: Be mindful of Allah, and He will protect you. Be mindful of Allah, and you will find Him before you.
Afraid.author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

Afraid.text: Know that if the whole nation gathered together to benefit you, they would not benefit you except with what Allah has already written for you. And if they gathered to harm you, they would not harm you except with what Allah has already written against you.
Afraid.author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

Afraid.text: Be eager for what benefits you, seek help from Allah, and do not lose heart.
Afraid.author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

Afraid.text: The strong believer is better and more beloved to Allah than the weak believer, though there is good in both.
Afraid.author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

Afraid.text: Tie your camel and then put your trust in Allah.
Afraid.author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

Afraid.text: Whoever says in the morning and evening: 'Hasbiya Allahu la ilaha illa Huwa...' seven times, Allah will suffice him in what concerns him.
Afraid.author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

Afraid.text: Ask Allah for well-being in this world and the Hereafter.
Afraid.author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

Afraid.text: O Allah, I seek refuge in You from anxiety and sorrow, weakness and laziness, miserliness and cowardice, the burden of debts, and being overpowered by men.
Afraid.author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

Afraid.text: Whoever relieves a believer of a hardship in this world, Allah will relieve him of a hardship on the Day of Resurrection.
Afraid.author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

Afraid.text: No fatigue, illness, anxiety, sorrow, harm, distress, or even the prick of a thorn befalls a Muslim except that Allah expiates some of his sins because of it.
Afraid.author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

Afraid.text: Whoever remains patient, Allah will grant him patience.
Afraid.author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

Afraid.text: The most beloved words to Allah are four: SubhanAllah, Alhamdulillah, La ilaha illallah, and Allahu Akbar.
Afraid.author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

Afraid.text: Allah is more merciful to His servants than a mother is to her child.
Afraid.author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

Afraid.text: When you ask, ask Allah; when you seek help, seek help from Allah.
Afraid.author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

Afraid.text: The best provision is consciousness of Allah (taqwa).
Afraid.author: Quran

Regretful.text: Say, 'O My servants who have transgressed against themselves, do not despair of the mercy of Allah. Indeed, Allah forgives all sins.'
Regretful.author: Quran

Regretful.text: And turn to Allah in repentance, all of you, O believers, that you might succeed.
Regretful.author: Quran

Regretful.text: Indeed, Allah loves those who are constantly repentant and loves those who purify themselves.
Regretful.author: Quran

Regretful.text: Except for those who repent, believe, and do righteous deeds. For them Allah will replace their evil deeds with good.
Regretful.author: Quran

Regretful.text: Whoever does wrong or wrongs himself and then seeks Allah's forgiveness will find Allah Forgiving and Merciful.
Regretful.author: Quran

Regretful.text: Seek forgiveness of your Lord and then repent to Him. Indeed, my Lord is Merciful and Loving.
Regretful.author: Quran

Regretful.text: And whoever repents and does righteousness has truly turned to Allah in sincere repentance.
Regretful.author: Quran

Regretful.text: Indeed, the mercy of Allah is near to the doers of good.
Regretful.author: Quran

Regretful.text: My mercy encompasses all things.
Regretful.author: Quran

Regretful.text: Indeed, Allah accepts the repentance of those who do evil in ignorance and then repent soon after.
Regretful.author: Quran

Regretful.text: Ask forgiveness of your Lord. Indeed, He is ever a Perpetual Forgiver.
Regretful.author: Quran

Regretful.text: He is the One who accepts repentance from His servants and pardons misdeeds.
Regretful.author: Quran

Regretful.text: Indeed, Allah is Forgiving and Merciful.
Regretful.author: Quran

Regretful.text: Our Lord, we have wronged ourselves. If You do not forgive us and have mercy upon us, we will surely be among the losers.
Regretful.author: Quran

Regretful.text: Indeed, Allah loves those who constantly repent.
Regretful.author: Quran

Regretful.text: Every son of Adam sins, and the best of those who sin are those who repent.
Regretful.author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

Regretful.text: Allah is more pleased with the repentance of His servant than one who finds his lost camel in the desert.
Regretful.author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

Regretful.text: By Allah, I seek Allah's forgiveness and repent to Him more than seventy times a day.
Regretful.author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

Regretful.text: Allah stretches out His Hand during the night to accept the repentance of the one who sinned during the day, and during the day to accept the repentance of the one who sinned during the night.
Regretful.author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

Regretful.text: The one who repents from sin is like one who never sinned.
Regretful.author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ
Regretful.source: Sunan Ibn Majah 4250 (graded Hasan by many scholars)

Regretful.text: Whoever says 'Astaghfirullah wa atubu ilayh' sincerely, Allah will forgive him.
Regretful.author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ
Regretful.source: Based on authentic narrations encouraging frequent istighfar

Regretful.text: Our Lord descends every night to the lowest heaven and says: 'Who is seeking My forgiveness so that I may forgive him?'
Regretful.author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

Regretful.text: Allah says: 'O son of Adam, were your sins to reach the clouds of the sky and then you sought My forgiveness, I would forgive you.'
Regretful.author: Allah (Hadith Qudsi)

Regretful.text: Whoever repents before the sun rises from the west, Allah will accept his repentance.
Regretful.author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

Regretful.text: The best among you are those who have the best character.
Regretful.author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

Regretful.text: Follow a bad deed with a good one; it will erase it.
Regretful.author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

Regretful.text: Fear Allah wherever you are, follow a bad deed with a good deed to erase it, and treat people with good character.
Regretful.author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

Regretful.text: Whoever seeks forgiveness for the believing men and women, Allah will record for him a good deed for every believing man and woman.
Regretful.author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

Regretful.text: Indeed, Allah accepts the repentance of a servant so long as the soul has not reached the throat.
Regretful.author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

Regretful.text: The doors of repentance remain open until the sun rises from the west.
Regretful.author: Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

Happy.text: Rejoice in the Lord alway: and again I say, Rejoice.
Happy.author: Bible

Happy.text: This is the day which the Lord hath made; we will rejoice and be glad in it.
Happy.author: Bible

Happy.text: The joy of the LORD is your strength.
Happy.author: Bible

Happy.text: Rejoice evermore.
Happy.author: Bible

Happy.text: Give thanks unto the LORD; for he is good: because his mercy endureth for ever.
Happy.author: Bible

Happy.text: O taste and see that the LORD is good: blessed is the man that trusteth in him.
Happy.author: Bible

Happy.text: Delight thyself also in the LORD; and he shall give thee the desires of thine heart.
Happy.author: Bible

Happy.text: Bless the LORD, O my soul: and all that is within me, bless his holy name.
Happy.author: Bible

Happy.text: Make a joyful noise unto the LORD, all ye lands.
Happy.author: Bible

Happy.text: Serve the LORD with gladness: come before his presence with singing.
Happy.author: Bible

Happy.text: The LORD is my shepherd; I shall not want.
Happy.author: Bible

Happy.text: The LORD is my light and my salvation; whom shall I fear?
Happy.author: Bible

Happy.text: Be glad in the LORD, and rejoice, ye righteous.
Happy.author: Bible

Happy.text: The blessing of the LORD, it maketh rich, and he addeth no sorrow with it.
Happy.author: Bible

Happy.text: A merry heart doeth good like a medicine.
Happy.author: Bible

Happy.text: Every good gift and every perfect gift is from above.
Happy.author: Bible

Happy.text: Let the peace of God rule in your hearts; and be ye thankful.
Happy.author: Bible

Happy.text: In every thing give thanks.
Happy.author: Bible

Happy.text: The fruit of the Spirit is love, joy, peace, longsuffering, gentleness, goodness, faith.
Happy.author: Bible

Happy.text: The kingdom of God is... righteousness, and peace, and joy in the Holy Ghost.
Happy.author: Bible

Happy.text: Happy is that people, whose God is the LORD.
Happy.author: Bible

Happy.text: Happy is he that hath the God of Jacob for his help.
Happy.author: Bible

Happy.text: These things have I spoken unto you, that my joy might remain in you, and that your joy might be full.
Happy.author: Jesus Christ

Happy.text: Ask, and ye shall receive, that your joy may be full.
Happy.author: Jesus Christ

Happy.text: Peace I leave with you, my peace I give unto you.
Happy.author: Jesus Christ

Happy.text: Come unto me, all ye that labour and are heavy laden, and I will give you rest.
Happy.author: Jesus Christ

Happy.text: Let your light so shine before men.
Happy.author: Jesus Christ

Happy.text: Ye are the light of the world.
Happy.author: Jesus Christ

Happy.text: With God all things are possible.
Happy.author: Jesus Christ

Happy.text: Fear not, little flock; for it is your Father's good pleasure to give you the kingdom.
Happy.author: Jesus Christ

Sad.text: Come unto me, all ye that labour and are heavy laden, and I will give you rest.
Sad.author: Jesus Christ

Sad.text: Blessed are they that mourn: for they shall be comforted.
Sad.author: Jesus Christ

Sad.text: Peace I leave with you, my peace I give unto you: not as the world giveth, give I unto you. Let not your heart be troubled, neither let it be afraid.
Sad.author: Jesus Christ

Sad.text: Let not your heart be troubled: ye believe in God, believe also in me.
Sad.author: Jesus Christ

Sad.text: These things I have spoken unto you, that in me ye might have peace. In the world ye shall have tribulation: but be of good cheer; I have overcome the world.
Sad.author: Jesus Christ

Sad.text: The LORD is nigh unto them that are of a broken heart; and saveth such as be of a contrite spirit.
Sad.author: Bible

Sad.text: Cast thy burden upon the LORD, and he shall sustain thee.
Sad.author: Bible

Sad.text: God is our refuge and strength, a very present help in trouble.
Sad.author: Bible

Sad.text: Be still, and know that I am God.
Sad.author: Bible

Sad.text: The LORD is my shepherd; I shall not want.
Sad.author: Bible

Sad.text: Yea, though I walk through the valley of the shadow of death, I will fear no evil: for thou art with me.
Sad.author: Bible

Sad.text: He healeth the broken in heart, and bindeth up their wounds.
Sad.author: Bible

Sad.text: The eternal God is thy refuge, and underneath are the everlasting arms.
Sad.author: Bible

Sad.text: Fear thou not; for I am with thee: be not dismayed; for I am thy God.
Sad.author: Bible

Sad.text: When thou passest through the waters, I will be with thee.
Sad.author: Bible

Sad.text: For I know the thoughts that I think toward you... thoughts of peace, and not of evil, to give you an expected end.
Sad.author: Bible

Sad.text: Weeping may endure for a night, but joy cometh in the morning.
Sad.author: Bible

Sad.text: The LORD also will be a refuge for the oppressed, a refuge in times of trouble.
Sad.author: Bible

Sad.text: Casting all your care upon him; for he careth for you.
Sad.author: Bible

Sad.text: My grace is sufficient for thee: for my strength is made perfect in weakness.
Sad.author: Jesus Christ

Sad.text: And we know that all things work together for good to them that love God.
Sad.author: Bible

Sad.text: For I am persuaded, that neither death, nor life... shall be able to separate us from the love of God.
Sad.author: Bible

Sad.text: Be careful for nothing; but in every thing by prayer and supplication with thanksgiving let your requests be made known unto God.
Sad.author: Bible

Sad.text: And the peace of God, which passeth all understanding, shall keep your hearts and minds through Christ Jesus.
Sad.author: Bible

Sad.text: The LORD is good, a strong hold in the day of trouble; and he knoweth them that trust in him.
Sad.author: Bible

Sad.text: Though he fall, he shall not be utterly cast down: for the LORD upholdeth him with his hand.
Sad.author: Bible

Sad.text: The righteous cry, and the LORD heareth, and delivereth them out of all their troubles.
Sad.author: Bible

Sad.text: Blessed be God... the Father of mercies, and the God of all comfort.
Sad.author: Bible

Sad.text: Who comforteth us in all our tribulation.
Sad.author: Bible

Sad.text: I will not leave you comfortless: I will come to you.
Sad.author: Jesus Christ

Lost.text: Jesus saith unto him, I am the way, the truth, and the life: no man cometh unto the Father, but by me.
Lost.author: Jesus Christ

Lost.text: Trust in the LORD with all thine heart; and lean not unto thine own understanding. In all thy ways acknowledge him, and he shall direct thy paths.
Lost.author: Bible

Lost.text: The LORD is my shepherd; I shall not want.
Lost.author: Bible

Lost.text: Thy word is a lamp unto my feet, and a light unto my path.
Lost.author: Bible

Lost.text: The steps of a good man are ordered by the LORD.
Lost.author: Bible

Lost.text: Commit thy way unto the LORD; trust also in him; and he shall bring it to pass.
Lost.author: Bible

Lost.text: I will instruct thee and teach thee in the way which thou shalt go: I will guide thee with mine eye.
Lost.author: Bible

Lost.text: The LORD shall guide thee continually.
Lost.author: Bible

Lost.text: For I know the thoughts that I think toward you... thoughts of peace, and not of evil, to give you an expected end.
Lost.author: Bible

Lost.text: Seek ye first the kingdom of God, and his righteousness; and all these things shall be added unto you.
Lost.author: Jesus Christ

Lost.text: Ask, and it shall be given you; seek, and ye shall find; knock, and it shall be opened unto you.
Lost.author: Jesus Christ

Lost.text: If any of you lack wisdom, let him ask of God... and it shall be given him.
Lost.author: Bible

Lost.text: The fear of the LORD is the beginning of wisdom.
Lost.author: Bible

Lost.text: Delight thyself also in the LORD; and he shall give thee the desires of thine heart.
Lost.author: Bible

Lost.text: Commit thy works unto the LORD, and thy thoughts shall be established.
Lost.author: Bible

Lost.text: A man's heart deviseth his way: but the LORD directeth his steps.
Lost.author: Bible

Lost.text: For the Son of man is come to seek and to save that which was lost.
Lost.author: Jesus Christ

Lost.text: I am the good shepherd: the good shepherd giveth his life for the sheep.
Lost.author: Jesus Christ

Lost.text: My sheep hear my voice, and I know them, and they follow me.
Lost.author: Jesus Christ

Lost.text: Come unto me, all ye that labour and are heavy laden, and I will give you rest.
Lost.author: Jesus Christ

Lost.text: If God be for us, who can be against us?
Lost.author: Bible

Lost.text: And we know that all things work together for good to them that love God.
Lost.author: Bible

Lost.text: Casting all your care upon him; for he careth for you.
Lost.author: Bible

Lost.text: The LORD is my light and my salvation; whom shall I fear?
Lost.author: Bible

Lost.text: Be strong and of a good courage... for the LORD thy God is with thee whithersoever thou goest.
Lost.author: Bible

Lost.text: The LORD is nigh unto all them that call upon him.
Lost.author: Bible

Lost.text: The entrance of thy words giveth light; it giveth understanding unto the simple.
Lost.author: Bible

Lost.text: And ye shall seek me, and find me, when ye shall search for me with all your heart.
Lost.author: Bible

Lost.text: Draw nigh to God, and he will draw nigh to you.
Lost.author: Bible

Lost.text: The LORD redeemeth the soul of his servants: and none of them that trust in him shall be desolate.
Lost.author: Bible

Lonely.text: I will never leave thee, nor forsake thee.
Lonely.author: Bible

Lonely.text: Be strong and of a good courage... for the LORD thy God is with thee whithersoever thou goest.
Lonely.author: Bible

Lonely.text: Fear thou not; for I am with thee: be not dismayed; for I am thy God.
Lonely.author: Bible

Lonely.text: When thou passest through the waters, I will be with thee.
Lonely.author: Bible

Lonely.text: The LORD is nigh unto them that are of a broken heart.
Lonely.author: Bible

Lonely.text: The LORD is nigh unto all them that call upon him.
Lonely.author: Bible

Lonely.text: Cast thy burden upon the LORD, and he shall sustain thee.
Lonely.author: Bible

Lonely.text: God is our refuge and strength, a very present help in trouble.
Lonely.author: Bible

Lonely.text: Yea, though I walk through the valley of the shadow of death, I will fear no evil: for thou art with me.
Lonely.author: Bible

Lonely.text: The eternal God is thy refuge, and underneath are the everlasting arms.
Lonely.author: Bible

Lonely.text: Come unto me, all ye that labour and are heavy laden, and I will give you rest.
Lonely.author: Jesus Christ

Lonely.text: I will not leave you comfortless: I will come to you.
Lonely.author: Jesus Christ

Lonely.text: Peace I leave with you, my peace I give unto you.
Lonely.author: Jesus Christ

Lonely.text: Abide in me, and I in you.
Lonely.author: Jesus Christ

Lonely.text: Lo, I am with you alway, even unto the end of the world.
Lonely.author: Jesus Christ

Lonely.text: Greater love hath no man than this, that a man lay down his life for his friends.
Lonely.author: Jesus Christ

Lonely.text: Ye are my friends, if ye do whatsoever I command you.
Lonely.author: Jesus Christ

Lonely.text: Casting all your care upon him; for he careth for you.
Lonely.author: Bible

Lonely.text: Nothing shall be able to separate us from the love of God.
Lonely.author: Bible

Lonely.text: If God be for us, who can be against us?
Lonely.author: Bible

Lonely.text: Bear ye one another's burdens, and so fulfil the law of Christ.
Lonely.author: Bible

Lonely.text: A friend loveth at all times.
Lonely.author: Bible

Lonely.text: Two are better than one... For if they fall, the one will lift up his fellow.
Lonely.author: Bible

Lonely.text: There is a friend that sticketh closer than a brother.
Lonely.author: Bible

Lonely.text: The LORD is good, a strong hold in the day of trouble; and he knoweth them that trust in him.
Lonely.author: Bible

Lonely.text: Blessed be God... the Father of mercies, and the God of all comfort.
Lonely.author: Bible

Lonely.text: Who comforteth us in all our tribulation.
Lonely.author: Bible

Lonely.text: Draw nigh to God, and he will draw nigh to you.
Lonely.author: Bible

Lonely.text: The LORD also will be a refuge for the oppressed, a refuge in times of trouble.
Lonely.author: Bible

Lonely.text: Behold, how good and how pleasant it is for brethren to dwell together in unity!
Lonely.author: Bible

Angry.text: Blessed are the peacemakers: for they shall be called the children of God.
Angry.author: Jesus Christ

Angry.text: Love your enemies, bless them that curse you, do good to them that hate you, and pray for them which despitefully use you.
Angry.author: Jesus Christ

Angry.text: Judge not, that ye be not judged.
Angry.author: Jesus Christ

Angry.text: Forgive, and ye shall be forgiven.
Angry.author: Jesus Christ

Angry.text: Father, forgive them; for they know not what they do.
Angry.author: Jesus Christ

Angry.text: A new commandment I give unto you, That ye love one another; as I have loved you.
Angry.author: Jesus Christ

Angry.text: By this shall all men know that ye are my disciples, if ye have love one to another.
Angry.author: Jesus Christ

Angry.text: Greater love hath no man than this, that a man lay down his life for his friends.
Angry.author: Jesus Christ

Angry.text: A soft answer turneth away wrath: but grievous words stir up anger.
Angry.author: Solomon

Angry.text: He that is slow to anger is better than the mighty.
Angry.author: Solomon

Angry.text: Hatred stirreth up strifes: but love covereth all sins.
Angry.author: Solomon

Angry.text: He that is slow to wrath is of great understanding.
Angry.author: Solomon

Angry.text: Cease from anger, and forsake wrath.
Angry.author: David

Angry.text: The LORD is merciful and gracious, slow to anger, and plenteous in mercy.
Angry.author: David

Angry.text: The discretion of a man deferreth his anger; and it is his glory to pass over a transgression.
Angry.author: Solomon

Angry.text: Be ye angry, and sin not: let not the sun go down upon your wrath.
Angry.author: Paul

Angry.text: Let all bitterness, and wrath, and anger... be put away from you.
Angry.author: Paul

Angry.text: And be ye kind one to another, tenderhearted, forgiving one another, even as God for Christ's sake hath forgiven you.
Angry.author: Paul

Angry.text: If it be possible, as much as lieth in you, live peaceably with all men.
Angry.author: Paul

Angry.text: Be not overcome of evil, but overcome evil with good.
Angry.author: Paul

Angry.text: Dearly beloved, avenge not yourselves.
Angry.author: Paul

Angry.text: The fruit of the Spirit is love, joy, peace, longsuffering, gentleness, goodness, faith, meekness, temperance.
Angry.author: Paul

Angry.text: Put on therefore... kindness, humbleness of mind, meekness, longsuffering.
Angry.author: Paul

Angry.text: Above all these things put on charity, which is the bond of perfectness.
Angry.author: Paul

Angry.text: Let the peace of God rule in your hearts.
Angry.author: Paul

Angry.text: Be ye therefore followers of God, as dear children; And walk in love.
Angry.author: Paul

Angry.text: Wherefore, my beloved brethren, let every man be swift to hear, slow to speak, slow to wrath.
Angry.author: James

Angry.text: For the wrath of man worketh not the righteousness of God.
Angry.author: James

Angry.text: Above all things have fervent charity among yourselves: for charity shall cover the multitude of sins.
Angry.author: Peter

Angry.text: Beloved, let us love one another: for love is of God.
Angry.author: John

Afraid.text: Fear thou not; for I am with thee: be not dismayed; for I am thy God.
Afraid.author: Bible

Afraid.text: Be strong and of a good courage; be not afraid... for the LORD thy God is with thee whithersoever thou goest.
Afraid.author: Bible

Afraid.text: The LORD is my light and my salvation; whom shall I fear?
Afraid.author: David

Afraid.text: Yea, though I walk through the valley of the shadow of death, I will fear no evil: for thou art with me.
Afraid.author: David

Afraid.text: God is our refuge and strength, a very present help in trouble.
Afraid.author: Sons of Korah

Afraid.text: Be still, and know that I am God.
Afraid.author: Bible

Afraid.text: What time I am afraid, I will trust in thee.
Afraid.author: David

Afraid.text: In God have I put my trust: I will not fear what flesh can do unto me.
Afraid.author: David

Afraid.text: The LORD is on my side; I will not fear.
Afraid.author: Bible

Afraid.text: The angel of the LORD encampeth round about them that fear him, and delivereth them.
Afraid.author: David

Afraid.text: Cast thy burden upon the LORD, and he shall sustain thee.
Afraid.author: David

Afraid.text: When thou passest through the waters, I will be with thee.
Afraid.author: Bible

Afraid.text: For the LORD thy God will hold thy right hand, saying unto thee, Fear not; I will help thee.
Afraid.author: Bible

Afraid.text: Have not I commanded thee? Be strong and of a good courage.
Afraid.author: Bible

Afraid.text: I sought the LORD, and he heard me, and delivered me from all my fears.
Afraid.author: David

Afraid.text: Peace I leave with you, my peace I give unto you... Let not your heart be troubled, neither let it be afraid.
Afraid.author: Jesus Christ

Afraid.text: Let not your heart be troubled: ye believe in God, believe also in me.
Afraid.author: Jesus Christ

Afraid.text: These things I have spoken unto you, that in me ye might have peace... be of good cheer; I have overcome the world.
Afraid.author: Jesus Christ

Afraid.text: Fear not, little flock; for it is your Father's good pleasure to give you the kingdom.
Afraid.author: Jesus Christ

Afraid.text: Be not afraid, only believe.
Afraid.author: Jesus Christ

Afraid.text: It is I; be not afraid.
Afraid.author: Jesus Christ

Afraid.text: Lo, I am with you alway, even unto the end of the world.
Afraid.author: Jesus Christ

Afraid.text: Casting all your care upon him; for he careth for you.
Afraid.author: Peter

Afraid.text: For God hath not given us the spirit of fear; but of power, and of love, and of a sound mind.
Afraid.author: Paul

Afraid.text: If God be for us, who can be against us?
Afraid.author: Paul

Afraid.text: Nothing shall be able to separate us from the love of God.
Afraid.author: Paul

Afraid.text: Be careful for nothing; but in every thing by prayer and supplication with thanksgiving let your requests be made known unto God.
Afraid.author: Paul

Afraid.text: And the peace of God, which passeth all understanding, shall keep your hearts and minds through Christ Jesus.
Afraid.author: Paul

Afraid.text: There is no fear in love; but perfect love casteth out fear.
Afraid.author: John

Afraid.text: The LORD is faithful, who shall stablish you, and keep you from evil.
Afraid.author: Paul

Regretful.text: Come unto me, all ye that labour and are heavy laden, and I will give you rest.
Regretful.author: Jesus Christ

Regretful.text: For the Son of man is come to seek and to save that which was lost.
Regretful.author: Jesus Christ

Regretful.text: Neither do I condemn thee: go, and sin no more.
Regretful.author: Jesus Christ

Regretful.text: There is joy in the presence of the angels of God over one sinner that repenteth.
Regretful.author: Jesus Christ

Regretful.text: Him that cometh to me I will in no wise cast out.
Regretful.author: Jesus Christ

Regretful.text: Repent ye, and believe the gospel.
Regretful.author: Jesus Christ

Regretful.text: If we confess our sins, he is faithful and just to forgive us our sins, and to cleanse us from all unrighteousness.
Regretful.author: John

Regretful.text: As far as the east is from the west, so far hath he removed our transgressions from us.
Regretful.author: David

Regretful.text: The LORD is merciful and gracious, slow to anger, and plenteous in mercy.
Regretful.author: David

Regretful.text: Create in me a clean heart, O God; and renew a right spirit within me.
Regretful.author: David

Regretful.text: The sacrifices of God are a broken spirit: a broken and a contrite heart... thou wilt not despise.
Regretful.author: David

Regretful.text: Have mercy upon me, O God, according to thy lovingkindness.
Regretful.author: David

Regretful.text: He restoreth my soul.
Regretful.author: David

Regretful.text: The LORD is nigh unto them that are of a broken heart.
Regretful.author: David

Regretful.text: Though he fall, he shall not be utterly cast down: for the LORD upholdeth him with his hand.
Regretful.author: David

Regretful.text: Let the wicked forsake his way... and let him return unto the LORD, and he will have mercy upon him.
Regretful.author: Bible

Regretful.text: Though your sins be as scarlet, they shall be as white as snow.
Regretful.author: Bible

Regretful.text: Return unto me... and I will return unto you.
Regretful.author: Bible

Regretful.text: For I will forgive their iniquity, and I will remember their sin no more.
Regretful.author: Bible

Regretful.text: A new heart also will I give you, and a new spirit will I put within you.
Regretful.author: Bible

Regretful.text: There is therefore now no condemnation to them which are in Christ Jesus.
Regretful.author: Paul

Regretful.text: Where sin abounded, grace did much more abound.
Regretful.author: Paul

Regretful.text: But God commendeth his love toward us, in that, while we were yet sinners, Christ died for us.
Regretful.author: Paul

Regretful.text: If any man be in Christ, he is a new creature: old things are passed away; behold, all things are become new.
Regretful.author: Paul

Regretful.text: For by grace are ye saved through faith.
Regretful.author: Paul

Regretful.text: In whom we have redemption through his blood, the forgiveness of sins.
Regretful.author: Paul

Regretful.text: The Lord is not willing that any should perish, but that all should come to repentance.
Regretful.author: Peter

Regretful.text: Humble yourselves therefore under the mighty hand of God, that he may exalt you in due time.
Regretful.author: Peter

Regretful.text: Blessed is he whose transgression is forgiven, whose sin is covered.
Regretful.author: David

Regretful.text: The LORD redeemeth the soul of his servants: and none of them that trust in him shall be desolate.
Regretful.author: David

happy.text: The one who is content in the Self, satisfied in the Self, and fulfilled in the Self has nothing further to achieve.
happy.author: Lord Krishna

happy.text: One whose mind is undisturbed in happiness and distress is fit for immortality.
happy.author: Lord Krishna

happy.text: He who is free from attachment, fear, and anger is dear to Me.
happy.author: Lord Krishna

happy.text: The peaceful person, whose mind is disciplined, attains supreme happiness.
happy.author: Lord Krishna

happy.text: From peace comes the end of all sorrow.
happy.author: Lord Krishna

happy.text: Whatever you do, whatever you eat, whatever you offer or give, do it as an offering unto Me.
happy.author: Lord Krishna

happy.text: To those who are constantly devoted to Me with love, I give the understanding by which they come to Me.
happy.author: Lord Krishna

happy.text: The yogi who is united with the Self sees the Self in all beings and all beings in the Self.
happy.author: Lord Krishna

happy.text: Fix your mind on Me, be devoted to Me, worship Me, and bow to Me.
happy.author: Lord Krishna

happy.text: Abandon all forms of duty and take refuge in Me alone. I shall free you from all sin; do not grieve.
happy.author: Lord Krishna

happy.text: He who is satisfied with whatever comes by chance is free from envy and remains balanced in success and failure.
happy.author: Lord Krishna

happy.text: The wise see with equal vision a learned person, a cow, an elephant, a dog, and one who eats dogs.
happy.author: Lord Krishna

happy.text: One who delights in the welfare of all beings attains peace.
happy.author: Lord Krishna

happy.text: Perform your duty with equanimity, abandoning attachment to success and failure.
happy.author: Lord Krishna

happy.text: Yoga is excellence in action.
happy.author: Lord Krishna

happy.text: Truth alone triumphs.
happy.author: Mundaka Upanishad

happy.text: Lead me from the unreal to the Real; from darkness to Light; from death to Immortality.
happy.author: Brihadaranyaka Upanishad

happy.text: May we hear what is auspicious with our ears; may we see what is auspicious with our eyes.
happy.author: Rig Veda

happy.text: Let noble thoughts come to us from every side.
happy.author: Rig Veda

happy.text: May all be happy; may all be free from illness; may all see what is auspicious; may none suffer.
happy.author: Traditional Vedic Prayer
happy.source: Brihadaranyaka Upanishad 1.4.14 (commonly recited as 'Sarve Bhavantu Sukhinah')

happy.text: The whole universe is pervaded by the Lord. Enjoy through renunciation; do not covet what belongs to another.
happy.author: Isha Upanishad

happy.text: The Self is bliss.
happy.author: Taittiriya Upanishad

happy.text: He who knows Brahman attains the Supreme.
happy.author: Taittiriya Upanishad

happy.text: The knower of the Self crosses beyond sorrow.
happy.author: Chandogya Upanishad

happy.text: May we be protected together. May we be nourished together. May we work together with great energy.
happy.author: Taittiriya Upanishad

happy.text: One who has conquered the mind finds peace in heat and cold, pleasure and pain, honor and dishonor.
happy.author: Lord Krishna

happy.text: He who is devoted with faith is dear to Me.
happy.author: Lord Krishna

happy.text: Among thousands of people, one strives for perfection; among those who strive, one truly knows Me.
happy.author: Lord Krishna

happy.text: The wise, established in yoga, abandon attachment to the fruits of action and attain lasting peace.
happy.author: Lord Krishna

happy.text: The one who rejoices within, is illuminated within, and is happy within attains liberation.
happy.author: Lord Krishna

sad.text: The wise grieve neither for the living nor for the dead.
sad.author: Lord Krishna

sad.text: The unreal has no existence; the real never ceases to be.
sad.author: Lord Krishna

sad.text: Just as a person casts off worn-out garments and puts on new ones, so the embodied soul casts off worn-out bodies and enters new ones.
sad.author: Lord Krishna

sad.text: The soul is never born, nor does it ever die.
sad.author: Lord Krishna

sad.text: Contact with the objects of the senses gives rise to cold and heat, pleasure and pain; they come and go and are impermanent. Endure them patiently.
sad.author: Lord Krishna

sad.text: One whose mind remains undisturbed in sorrow and free from craving in happiness is called a sage of steady wisdom.
sad.author: Lord Krishna

sad.text: From peace comes the end of all sorrow.
sad.author: Lord Krishna

sad.text: The peaceful person, whose mind is disciplined, attains supreme happiness.
sad.author: Lord Krishna

sad.text: The one who rejoices within, who is illuminated within, attains liberation and the peace of Brahman.
sad.author: Lord Krishna

sad.text: Perform your duty with equanimity, abandoning attachment to success and failure.
sad.author: Lord Krishna

sad.text: He who is content with whatever comes by chance remains free from sorrow.
sad.author: Lord Krishna

sad.text: The Self cannot be cut by weapons, burned by fire, wetted by water, or dried by the wind.
sad.author: Lord Krishna

sad.text: Fix your mind on Me alone; by My grace you shall overcome every difficulty.
sad.author: Lord Krishna

sad.text: Abandon all forms of duty and take refuge in Me alone. I shall free you from all sin; do not grieve.
sad.author: Lord Krishna

sad.text: The one who has conquered the mind finds peace amid pleasure and pain.
sad.author: Lord Krishna

sad.text: Lead me from the unreal to the Real; from darkness to Light; from death to Immortality.
sad.author: Brihadaranyaka Upanishad

sad.text: The knower of the Self crosses beyond sorrow.
sad.author: Chandogya Upanishad

sad.text: The Self is bliss.
sad.author: Taittiriya Upanishad

sad.text: Truth alone triumphs.
sad.author: Mundaka Upanishad

sad.text: May we be protected together. May we be nourished together. May we work together with great energy.
sad.author: Taittiriya Upanishad

sad.text: One who sees all beings in the Self and the Self in all beings never turns away from it.
sad.author: Isha Upanishad

sad.text: Where there is duality, one sees another; but where everything has become the Self, what sorrow can remain?
sad.author: Isha Upanishad

sad.text: The whole universe is pervaded by the Lord. Enjoy through renunciation.
sad.author: Isha Upanishad

sad.text: Let noble thoughts come to us from every side.
sad.author: Rig Veda

sad.text: May all be happy; may all be free from illness; may all see what is auspicious; may none suffer.
sad.author: Traditional Vedic Prayer

sad.text: He who delights in the welfare of all beings attains peace.
sad.author: Lord Krishna

sad.text: The yogi whose mind is at peace sees the Divine in every being.
sad.author: Lord Krishna

sad.text: To those who are constantly devoted to Me with love, I give the understanding by which they come to Me.
sad.author: Lord Krishna

sad.text: Those who worship Me with unwavering devotion are exceedingly dear to Me.
sad.author: Lord Krishna

sad.text: The person established in yoga remains balanced in success and failure.
sad.author: Lord Krishna

lost.text: Fix your mind on Me, be devoted to Me, worship Me, and bow to Me. Thus you shall surely come to Me.
lost.author: Lord Krishna

lost.text: Abandon all forms of duty and take refuge in Me alone. I shall liberate you from all sin; do not grieve.
lost.author: Lord Krishna

lost.text: To those who are constantly devoted to Me with love, I give the understanding by which they come to Me.
lost.author: Lord Krishna

lost.text: Out of compassion for them, I dwell in their hearts and dispel the darkness born of ignorance with the shining lamp of knowledge.
lost.author: Lord Krishna

lost.text: Perform your prescribed duty, for action is better than inaction.
lost.author: Lord Krishna

lost.text: You have a right to perform your duty, but not to the fruits of your actions.
lost.author: Lord Krishna

lost.text: Yoga is excellence in action.

Angry.text: From anger comes delusion; from delusion, confused memory; from confused memory, the destruction of intelligence; and from the destruction of intelligence one perishes.
Angry.author: Lord Krishna

Angry.text: While contemplating the objects of the senses, attachment arises. From attachment comes desire, and from desire comes anger.

Angry.text: From peace comes the end of all sorrow.

Regretful.text: He soon becomes righteous and attains lasting peace. Declare it boldly: My devotee never perishes.

happy: Let this light stay with you; {figure} would remind you that joy grows when it is shared.
{figure} points toward a simple truth: this good moment is worth noticing fully.
Carry your smile forward; even a small gladness can change the room around you.
{figure} would say that gratitude turns an ordinary day into a remembered one.
You are allowed to enjoy this without apology. Let happiness breathe.
The heart knows when it has found a spark. Protect this one gently.
{figure} teaches that celebration is not escape; it is strength returning.
This is proof that lighter days can find you again.
Let the good feeling become fuel for kindness, courage, and clear choices.
{figure} would tell you to laugh honestly and keep walking.
A bright mood is a doorway. Step through it with both feet.
Keep this warmth close; it may become someone else's hope later.
{figure} reminds you that wonder is still available in ordinary places.
The day is answering you kindly. Answer it back.
Joy does not need to be loud to be real.
{figure} would call this a moment to trust your own aliveness.
Let yourself be easy to delight today.
This happiness is not fragile; it is practice for believing in tomorrow.
{figure} would say the best victories are the ones that make the heart softer.
You have earned this breath of brightness.
Notice what made you smile; it is a clue to what matters.
{figure} would remind you that hope often arrives disguised as a good mood.
Let the day have its music, and let yourself move with it.
Good energy becomes stronger when you spend it generously.
{figure} would say: keep the joy, but stay humble enough to pass it on.
A happy heart sees paths that fear cannot see.
This light belongs to you. Do not rush past it.
{figure} would celebrate the courage it took to reach this lighter place.
Let this feeling teach your mind that peace is possible.
The world feels wider when joy is present. Walk wide.
sad: {figure} would not ask you to hide this ache; feeling it is part of healing it.
Be gentle with yourself. Sadness is a weather, not your whole sky.
Even heavy hearts keep beating toward softer mornings.
{figure} reminds you that tears can be honest messengers, not failures.
You do not have to solve everything tonight. Just stay with the next breath.
The pain is real, but it is not the end of your story.
{figure} would sit beside the sorrow and wait for it to speak.
Let the sadness pass through you without letting it name you.
A low day still counts as a day you survived.
{figure} teaches that compassion begins where pretending stops.
Your heart is tired because it cared. That care is still beautiful.
Rest is a brave answer when the spirit feels bruised.
{figure} would say that brokenness can become a place where light enters.
You are not weak for needing comfort.
Some days ask for quiet courage. This is one of them.
{figure} reminds you that grief and love often hold the same hand.
Let yourself be held by small things: water, warmth, a steady breath.
The sadness may be loud, but your future is still speaking.
{figure} would honor the ache without building a home inside it.
There is no shame in being human all the way down.
A tender heart is not a broken instrument.
{figure} would say: take the next kind step, even if it is small.
You can be sad and still be moving toward light.
Do not measure your worth by today's heaviness.
{figure} reminds you that storms spend themselves eventually.
Your softness is not the problem; it is the place healing starts.
Let today be simple: breathe, drink water, keep going.
{figure} would call this pain a chapter, not a verdict.
You deserve patience while your heart finds its balance.
Even now, something in you is choosing to continue.
lost: {figure} would remind you that being lost is often how a deeper path begins.
You do not need the whole map. Find the next honest step.
Confusing roads can still lead to meaningful places.
{figure} teaches that direction returns to people who keep listening.
Pause. The path may be quiet because it is asking you to notice.
You are not behind; you are between answers.
{figure} would say that wandering can become wisdom if you stay awake.
When the way is unclear, choose what keeps you kind and steady.
A lost season is not a wasted season.
{figure} reminds you that the stars are most useful when the road goes dark.
Let uncertainty make you curious before it makes you afraid.
You can begin again from exactly where you are.
{figure} would trust the small signal over the loud panic.
Every map was once made by someone who did not know the route.
If you cannot see far, walk with care nearby.
{figure} would say that the question itself is carrying you forward.
You have found your way before. That memory belongs to you.
The path may be forming under your feet.
{figure} reminds you that clarity often arrives after honest movement.
Do not confuse a quiet chapter with an empty one.
Look for what still feels true. Start there.
{figure} would call this a threshold, not a dead end.
Being lost can loosen the life that no longer fits.
You are allowed to change direction without calling the past a failure.
{figure} would say: keep your values visible when the road is not.
The next step does not have to impress anyone. It only has to be real.
You are closer than you feel when you are still asking good questions.
{figure} reminds you that mystery is not the enemy of meaning.
Let the unknown teach you patience, not panic.
A new path often starts as a feeling you almost ignore.
lonely: {figure} would remind you that loneliness is a call for connection, not proof you are unlovable.
Your presence matters even when no one is close enough to say it.
Reach gently toward the world; one honest message can open a door.
{figure} teaches that solitude can hold you while connection finds its way back.
You are not too much, and you are not invisible.
Some rooms feel empty because your heart is ready for truer company.
{figure} would sit with you in the silence until it became less sharp.
Loneliness lies by making temporary distance sound permanent.
You belong to more than this moment.
{figure} reminds you that being seen begins with not abandoning yourself.
Let one small act of care be your bridge today.
The need for people is not weakness. It is part of being alive.
{figure} would say that a quiet heart is still worthy of warm company.
You can be alone right now and still deeply connected to life.
Do not let silence convince you that love has ended.
{figure} reminds you that friendship often begins with one brave reach.
Treat yourself like someone you are responsible for welcoming home.
The world is wider than the room you are in.
{figure} would tell you that your story still has people waiting in later chapters.
Even when no one notices, your tenderness is real.
Let the loneliness become a signal, not a sentence.
{figure} would say: begin with one kind contact, even if it feels small.
You deserve relationships where your spirit can rest.
A lonely evening cannot erase a lovable life.
{figure} reminds you that connection grows from honest openings.
Hold on to yourself until someone worthy can hold the conversation with you.
There is nothing embarrassing about needing warmth.
{figure} would call your longing sacred evidence that your heart still reaches.
You are not outside the circle forever.
Let tomorrow have the chance to bring a voice closer.
energetic: {figure} would tell you to aim this energy at something worthy and begin now.
Momentum is visiting. Give it a clear task.
Your fire is useful when your focus holds it.
{figure} reminds you that action turns belief into evidence.
Move with purpose; the day is ready to meet you.
This spark can become discipline if you give it rhythm.
{figure} would say that courage loves a moving body.
Use the rush, but keep your heart clean.
Energy becomes power when it serves a meaningful direction.
{figure} teaches that the first bold step changes the size of the road.
Let enthusiasm make you generous, not reckless.
The work will feel lighter if you start before doubt catches up.
{figure} would tell you to make the next hour count.
Your body knows something is possible. Listen.
Begin with the piece that makes everything else easier.
{figure} reminds you that greatness is often repeated effort with a bright face.
Turn the volume of your life up with intention.
You have enough charge for a brave beginning.
{figure} would say: do the thing while the door is open.
Let your energy build something your calmer self will thank you for.
Act with joy, then refine with patience.
{figure} reminds you that speed is best when guided by wisdom.
Your momentum is a gift. Spend it on what matters.
Take the shot, send the message, start the page.
{figure} would celebrate motion that carries others forward too.
The day has a pulse, and you are in rhythm with it.
Let action prove that you are ready.
{figure} would say that alive energy deserves a noble channel.
Do not wait for perfect. Begin with honest force.
Move, build, learn, repeat.
confused: {figure} would remind you that confusion is the mind making room for a better answer.
Slow down. A tangled thought often loosens with patience.
You do not need instant certainty to make a wise next move.
{figure} teaches that questions can be lanterns.
Name what you know, then gently circle what you do not.
Confusion is uncomfortable, but it is also proof you are thinking.
{figure} would ask for one clear breath before one clear choice.
Let the noise settle before you trust the signal.
You can be unsure and still be capable.
{figure} reminds you that clarity is built, not magically found.
Write down the question. It will become smaller outside your head.
When everything feels tangled, return to your values.
{figure} would say: do not rush the answer past its honesty.
The unknown is not mocking you. It is inviting attention.
Separate fear from fact, then begin again.
{figure} reminds you that wisdom often starts as humility.
Ask the next useful question, not every question at once.
Your mind is not broken; it is sorting.
{figure} would tell you to choose the kindest reasonable step.
A foggy morning can still become a clear afternoon.
Give yourself permission to learn out loud.
{figure} reminds you that uncertainty becomes lighter when shared.
Do not demand a final answer from a first draft of understanding.
Clarity likes quiet evidence.
{figure} would say that patient attention is its own form of courage.
The knot will not untie faster because you shame your hands.
Choose one thread. Follow it gently.
{figure} reminds you that not knowing can be the doorway to discovery.
You are allowed to pause before you decide.
Confusion is a bridge when you keep walking carefully.
